Description.1: Capacity factor of the wind farm
Description.10: Return on investment: ROI can also be expressed as BCR – 1. A higher ROI is more competitive. ROI ≥ 0 for economic viability.
Description.11: Profit margin: PM can also be expressed as 1 - CBR. A higher PM is more competitive. PM ≥ 0 for economic viability.
Description.1179: magnitude of wind speed at each z location
Description.12: Profitability adjusted PLCOE is the product of a benchmark price and CBR, which is equal to LCOE divided by value factor. A lower PLCOE is more competitive. PLCOE ≤ benchmark price for economic viability.
Description.1202: rotor axial induction at cut-in wind speed along blade span
Description.1203: rotor tangential induction at cut-in wind speed along blade span

Description.1208: lift coefficient distribution along blade span at cut-in wind speed
Description.1209: drag coefficient distribution along blade span at cut-in wind speed

Description.1224: Aerodynamic force coefficients at hub center in the hub c.s.
Description.1225: Aerodynamic moment coefficients at hub center in the hub c.s.

Description.1231: constraint for maximum strain in trailing edge pressure side

Description.1233: constraint on edge blade frequency such that ratio of 3P/f is above or below gamma with constraint <= 0

Description.358: Total blade material costs including the waste per blade.

Description.366: Total labor overhead cost per blade.
Description.368: Total blade fixed cost per blade (equipment, tooling, building, maintenance, labor, capital).

Description.404: spar cap, pressure side, boolean of materials in each composite layer spanwise, passed as floats for differentiablity, used for Fatigue Analysis

Description.45: Number of repair and maintenance requests submitted, canceled, not completed, and completed for each category.
Description.469: sectional fore-aft intertia per unit length about the Y_G inertia axis
Description.470: sectional side-side intertia per unit length about the Y_G inertia axis
Description.471: sectional fore-aft bending stiffness per unit length about the Y_E elastic axis
Description.472: sectional side-side bending stiffness per unit length about the Y_E elastic axis
Description.475: offset from the sectional center of mass
Description.476: offset from the sectional shear center
Description.477: offset from the sectional tension center
Description.5: Net value of capacity: NVOC is the difference in an asset’s total annualized value and annualized cost, divided by the installed capacity of the asset. NVOC ≥ 0 for economic viability.
Description.506: 1D array of the aerodynamic centers of each airfoil used along span.
Description.507: 1D array of the relative thicknesses of each airfoil used along span.
Description.508: 1D array of the angles of attack used to define the polars of the airfoils. All airfoils defined in openmdao share this grid.
Description.509: 1D array of the Reynolds numbers used to define the polars of the airfoils. All airfoils defined in openmdao share this grid.
Description.510: 4D array with the lift coefficients of the airfoils. Dimension 0 is along the different airfoils defined in the yaml, dimension 1 is along the angles of attack, dimension 2 is along the Reynolds number, dimension 3 is along the number of tabs, which may describe multiple sets at the same station, for example in presence of a flap.
Description.511: 4D array with the drag coefficients of the airfoils. Dimension 0 is along the different airfoils defined in the yaml, dimension 1 is along the angles of attack, dimension 2 is along the Reynolds number, dimension 3 is along the number of tabs, which may describe multiple sets at the same station, for example in presence of a flap.
Description.512: 4D array with the moment coefficients of the airfoils. Dimension 0 is along the different airfoils defined in the yaml, dimension 1 is along the angles of attack, dimension 2 is along the Reynolds number, dimension 3 is along the number of tabs, which may describe multiple sets at the same station, for example in presence of a flap.
Description.513: 3D array of the x and y airfoil coordinates of the n_af_master airfoils used along blade span.
Description.514: 2D array of the coordinates (x,y,z) of the blade reference axis, defined along blade span. The coordinate system is the one of BeamDyn: it is placed at blade root with x pointing the suction side of the blade, y pointing the trailing edge and z along the blade span. A standard configuration will have negative x values (prebend), if swept positive y values, and positive z values.
Description.515: 3D array of the dimensional x and y airfoil coordinates of the airfoils interpolated along span for n_span stations. The origin is placed at the pitch axis.
Description.516: 3D array of the dimensional x and y airfoil coordinates of the airfoils interpolated along span for n_span stations. The origin is placed at the pitch axis.
Description.517: The wetted (painted) surface area of the blade
Description.518: The projected surface area of the blade
Description.532: Scalar of the rotor diameter, defined as 2 x (Rhub + blade length along z) * cos(precone).
Description.533: 1D array of the dimensional spanwise grid defined along the rotor (hub radius to blade tip projected on the plane)
Description.534: Distance between rotor center and blade tip along z axis of the blade root c.s.
Description.535: 2D array of the coordinates (x,y,z) of the blade reference axis scaled based on rotor diameter, defined along blade span. The coordinate system is the one of BeamDyn: it is placed at blade root with x pointing the suction side of the blade, y pointing the trailing edge and z along the blade span. A standard configuration will have negative x values (prebend), if swept positive y values, and positive z values.
Description.540: Scalar of the 3D blade length computed along its axis, scaled based on the user defined rotor diameter.
Description.543: 1D array of the relative thicknesses of the blade defined along span.
Description.544: 1D array of the aerodynamic center of the blade defined along span.
Description.545: 4D array with the lift coefficients of the airfoils. Dimension 0 is along the blade span for n_span stations, dimension 1 is along the angles of attack, dimension 2 is along the Reynolds number.
Description.546: 4D array with the drag coefficients of the airfoils. Dimension 0 is along the blade span for n_span stations, dimension 1 is along the angles of attack, dimension 2 is along the Reynolds number.
Description.547: 4D array with the moment coefficients of the airfoils. Dimension 0 is along the blade span for n_span stations, dimension 1 is along the angles of attack, dimension 2 is along the Reynolds number.
Description.548: 3D array of the non-dimensional x and y airfoil coordinates of the airfoils interpolated along span for n_span stations. The leading edge is place at x=0 and y=0.
Description.590: 1D array of the non dimensional positions of the airfoils af_master defined along blade span.
Description.591: 1D array of the non-dimensional spanwise grid defined along blade axis (0-blade root, 1-blade tip)
Description.592: 1D array of the chord values defined along blade span.
Description.593: 1D array of the twist values defined along blade span. The twist is defined positive for negative rotations around the z axis (the same as in BeamDyn).
Description.594: 1D array of the airfoil position relative to the reference axis, specifying the distance in meters along the chordline from the reference axis to the leading edge. 0 means that the airfoil is pinned at the leading edge, a positive offset means that the leading edge is upstream of the reference axis in local chordline coordinates, and a negative offset that the leading edge aft of the reference axis.
Description.595: 1D array of the airfoil position relative to the reference axis, specifying the chordline normal distance in meters from the reference axis. 0 means that the reference axis lies on the airfoil chordline, a positive offset means that the chordline is shifted in the direction of the suction side relative to the reference axis, and a negative offset that the section is shifted in the direction of the pressure side of the airfoil.
Description.596: 1D array of the relative thickness values defined along blade span.
Description.597: 1D array of the twist values defined along blade span. The twist is the result of the parameterization.
Description.598: 1D array of the chord values defined along blade span. The chord is the result of the parameterization.
Description.599: 1D array of the ratio between chord values and maximum chord along blade span.
Description.6: Net value of energy: NVOE is the difference between LVOE and LCOE. NVOE ≥ 0 for economic viability.
Description.600: 1D array of the difference between one chord point and the other. It can be used as constraint to achieve monotically increasing and then decreasing chord
Description.601: 1D array of the difference between one twist point and the other. It can be used as constraint to achieve monotically decreasing and then increasing chord
Description.602: 2D array of the thickness of the layers of the blade structure after the parametrization. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents each entry along blade span.
Description.603: 2D array of the non-dimensional start point defined along the outer profile of a web. The TE suction side is 0, the TE pressure side is 1. The first dimension represents each web, the second dimension represents each entry along blade span.
Description.604: 2D array of the dimensional offset of a web with respect to the reference axis. The first dimension represents each web, the second dimension represents each entry along blade span.
Description.605: 1D array of the dimensional rotation of a web with respect to the reference axis. The dimension represents each web.
Description.606: 2D array of the non-dimensional end point defined along the outer profile of a web. The TE suction side is 0, the TE pressure side is 1. The first dimension represents each web, the second dimension represents each entry along blade span.
Description.607: 2D array of the thickness of the layers of the blade structure. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents span.
Description.608: 2D array of the start_nd_arc of the layers. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents span.
Description.609: 2D array of the end_nd_arc of the layers. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents span.
Description.610: 2D array of the width of the layers. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents span.
Description.611: 2D array of the dimensional offset of a layer with respect to the reference axis. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents each entry along blade span.
Description.612: 1D array of the dimensional rotation of a layer with respect to the reference axis. The dimension represents each layer.
Description.613: 2D array of the orientation of the layers of the blade structure. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents span.
Description.614: Spanwise position of a blade segmentation joint.
Description.615: Mass of the blade spanwise joint.
Description.617: Diameter of the blade root fastener.
Description.618: Max stress on each blade root bolt.
Description.619: 1D array of boolean values indicating whether to build a web from offset and rotation.
Description.620: 1D array of boolean values indicating how to build a layer.
Description.621: Index used to fix a layer to another
Description.622: Index used to fix a layer to another
Description.623: 2D array of the non-dimensional start point defined along the outer profile of a web. The TE suction side is 0, the TE pressure side is 1. The first dimension represents each web, the second dimension represents each entry along blade span.
Description.624: 2D array of the non-dimensional end point defined along the outer profile of a web. The TE suction side is 0, the TE pressure side is 1. The first dimension represents each web, the second dimension represents each entry along blade span.
Description.625: 2D array of the start_nd_arc of the layers. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents span.
Description.626: 2D array of the end_nd_arc of the layers. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents span.
Description.627: Distance between turbines in rotor diameters
Description.628: Distance between turbine rows in rotor diameters
Description.644: Electrical rated power of the generator.
Description.646: Diameter of the wind turbine rotor specified by the user, defined as 2 x (Rhub + blade length along z) * cos(precone).
Description.647: Height of the hub center over the ground (land-based) or the mean sea level (offshore) specified by the user.
Description.648: IEC wind turbine class. I - offshore, II coastal, III - land-based, IV - low wind speed site.
Description.649: IEC wind turbine category. A - high turbulence intensity (land-based), B - mid turbulence, C - low turbulence (offshore).
Description.651: Rotor orientation, either upwind or downwind.
Description.652: Convenient boolean for upwind (True) or downwind (False).
Description.653: Number of blades of the rotor.
Description.654: Cut in wind speed. This is the wind speed where region II begins.
Description.655: Cut out wind speed. This is the wind speed where region III ends.
Description.661: Constant tip speed ratio in region II.
Description.662: Constant pitch angle in region II.
Description.663: Scalar applied to the max thrust within RotorSE for peak thrust shaving.
Description.666: Average annual operational expenditures of the turbine
Description.667: The losses in AEP due to waked conditions
Description.668: Fixed charge rate for coe calculation
Description.696: Shaft uptilt angle. A standard machine has positive values.
Description.697: Vertical distance from tower top plane to hub flange
Description.698: Horizontal distance from tower top edge to hub flange
Description.699: Efficiency of the gearbox. Set to 1.0 for direct-drive
Description.7: System LCOE: SLCOE is the negative of NVOE but further adjusted by a benchmark price. System LCOE ≤ benchmark price for economic viability.
Description.701: User override of gearbox radius (only used if gearbox_mass_user is > 0).
Description.702: User override of gearbox length (only used if gearbox_mass_user is > 0).
Description.703: Total gear ratio of drivetrain (use 1.0 for direct)
Description.704: Distance from hub flange to first main bearing along shaft
Description.705: Distance from first to second main bearing along shaft
Description.708: Damping ratio for the drivetrain system
Description.709: Override regular regression-based calculation of brake mass with this value
Description.710: Regression-based scaling coefficient on machine rating to get HVAC system mass
Description.711: Override regular regression-based calculation of converter mass with this value
Description.712: Override regular regression-based calculation of transformer mass with this value
Description.713: Override regular regression-based calculation of first main bearing mass with this value
Description.714: Override regular regression-based calculation of second main bearing mass with this value
Description.715: Override bottom-up calculation of bedplate mass with this value
Description.716: Diameter of nose (also called turret or spindle)
Description.717: Thickness of nose (also called turret or spindle)
Description.725: Type of main bearing: CARB / CRB / SRB / TRB
Description.726: Type of main bearing: CARB / CRB / SRB / TRB
Description.727: If power electronics are located uptower (True) or at tower base (False)
Description.728: Material name identifier for the low speed shaft
Description.729: Material name identifier for the high speed shaft
Description.730: Material name identifier for the bedplate
Description.735: Shape parameter of the Weibull probability density function of the wind.
Description.738: Water depth for analysis.  Values > 0 mean offshore
Description.8: Benefit cost ratio: BCR is the discounted sum of total value divided by the discounted sum of total cost. A higher BCR is more competitive. BCR ≥ 1 for economic viability
Description.811: 2D array of the coordinates (x,y,z) of the tower reference axis. The coordinate system is the global coordinate system of OpenFAST: it is placed at tower base with x pointing downwind, y pointing on the side and z pointing vertically upwards. A standard tower configuration will have zero x and y values and positive z values.
Description.812: Height of the hub in the global reference system, i.e. distance rotor center to ground.
Description.813: Radius of the hub. It defines the distance of the blade root from the rotor center along the coned line.
Description.814: Cone angle of the rotor. It defines the angle between the rotor plane and the blade pitch axis. A standard machine has positive values.
Description.834: 1D array of the ply thicknesses of the materials. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.835: 1D array of the non-dimensional fiber volume fraction of the composite materials. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.836: 1D array of the non-dimensional fiber weight- fraction of the composite materials. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.837: 2D array of the Youngs moduli of the materials. Each row represents a material, the three columns represent E11, E22 and E33.
Description.838: 2D array of the shear moduli of the materials. Each row represents a material, the three columns represent G12, G13 and G23.
Description.839: 2D array of the Poisson ratio of the materials. Each row represents a material, the three columns represent nu12, nu13 and nu23.
Description.840: 2D array of the Ultimate Tensile Strength (UTS) of the materials. Each row represents a material, the three columns represent Xt12, Xt13 and Xt23.
Description.841: 2D array of the Ultimate Compressive Strength (UCS) of the materials. Each row represents a material, the three columns represent Xc12, Xc13 and Xc23.
Description.842: 2D array of the Ultimate Shear Strength (USS) of the materials. Each row represents a material, the three columns represent S12, S13 and S23.
Description.843: Yield stress of the material (in the principle direction for composites).
Description.844: Exponent of S-N Wohler fatigue curve in the form of S = A*N^-(1/m).
Description.845: Stress-intercept (A) of S-N Wohler fatigue curve in the form of S = A*N^-(1/m), taken as ultimate stress unless otherwise specified.
Description.846: 1D array of the unit costs of the materials.
Description.847: 1D array of the non-dimensional waste fraction of the materials.
Description.848: 1D array of the roll mass of the composite fabrics. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.849: 1D array of the density of the fibers of the materials.
Description.850: 1D array of the density of the materials. For composites, this is the density of the laminate.
Description.851: 1D array of the dry aerial density of the composite fabrics. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.852: 1D array of the ply thicknesses of the materials. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.853: 1D array of the non-dimensional fiber volume fraction of the composite materials. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.854: 1D array of the non-dimensional fiber weight- fraction of the composite materials. Non-composite materials are kept at 0.
Description.855: 1D array of flags to set whether a material is isotropic (0) or orthtropic (1). Each entry represents a material.
Description.857: 1D array of the non-dimensional grid defined along the tower axis (0-tower base, 1-tower top)
Description.858: Scalar of the tower height computed along the z axis.
Description.859: Scalar of the tower length computed along its curved axis. A standard straight tower will be as high as long.
Description.860: Foundation height in respect to the ground level.
Description.861: 1D array of the outer diameter values defined along the tower axis.
Description.862: 2D array of the thickness of the layers of the tower structure. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents each piecewise-constant entry of the tower sections.
Description.863: Multiplier that accounts for secondary structure mass inside of tower
Description.867: Override bottom-up calculation of total monopile mass with this value
Description.868: 1D array of the names of the layers modeled in the tower structure.
Description.869: 1D array of the names of the materials of each layer modeled in the tower structure.
Description.870: Distance, in km, that servicing equipment must travel daily to reach the wind farm
Description.871: Distance, in km, that tugboats must travel to reach the wind farm for tow-to-port repairs
Description.872: Reduced speed applied to servicing equipment in the reduced speed period
Description.873: Hour of the day where any work-related activities begin
Description.874: Hour of the day where any work-related activities end
Description.875: Number of crew transfer vessels that should be made available to the wind farm.
Description.876: Number of heavy lift vessels that should be made available to the wind farm (fixed-bottom simulations only)
Description.877: Number of tugboat groups that should be available to the port to tow floating turbines to port and back
Description.878: Hour of the day where any work-related activities begin for port-side repairs
Description.879: Hour of the day where any work-related activities end for port-side repairs
Description.880: Number of port-side crews available to work on simultaneous repairs for any at-port turbine
Description.881: Number of turbines that can be at port at once
Description.882: Date of first maintenance event to determine regular interval timing. Can be set to prior to the starting year to ensure staggered starts.
Description.883: Starting date, in MM/DD format, for an annual period where the site is inaccessible
Description.884: Ending date, in MM/DD format, for an annual period where the site is inaccessible
Description.885: Starting date, in MM/DD format, for an annual period where traveling speed is reduced
Description.886: Ending date, in MM/DD format, for an annual period where traveling speed is reduced
Description.887: Random seed for the internal random generator
Description.888: 2D array of the coordinates (x,y,z) of the tower reference axis. The coordinate system is the global coordinate system of OpenFAST: it is placed at tower base with x pointing downwind, y pointing on the side and z pointing vertically upwards. A standard tower configuration will have zero x and y values and positive z values.
Description.889: 1D array of the outer diameter values defined along the tower axis.
Description.890: 1D array of the drag coefficients defined along the tower height.
Description.891: 2D array of the thickness of the layers of the tower structure. The first dimension represents each layer, the second dimension represents each piecewise-constant entry of the tower sections.
Description.892: Multiplier that accounts for secondary structure mass inside of tower
Description.893: Override bottom-up calculation of total tower mass with this value
Description.894: 1D array of the lumped mass values defined along the tower axis.
Description.895: 1D array of the names of the layers modeled in the tower structure.
Description.896: 1D array of the names of the materials of each layer modeled in the tower structure.
Description.897: 1D array of the non-dimensional grid defined along the tower axis (0-tower base, 1-tower top)
Description.898: Scalar of the tower height computed along the z axis.
Description.899: Scalar of the tower length computed along its curved axis. A standard straight tower will be as high as long.
Description.9: Cost benefit ratio: CBR is the inverse of BCR. CBR ≤ 1 for economic viability. A lower CBR is more competitive.
Description.900: Foundation height in respect to the ground level.
